title: Overview
summary: The first and second resonance of tuning fork in resonance tube is shown below: <div style="text-align: center;"><img…
body:
**Key points from solved questions** - L.C. of the screw gauge = $ \frac{\text{Pitch}}{\text{Number of divisions on circular scale}} = \frac{1}{100} = 0.01 \, \text{mm} $ Given, circular scale reading = 0.01 × 63 = 0.63 mm ∴ Diameter of wire = Total reading = Main Scale reading + Circular Scale Reading = 1 mm + 0.63 mm = 1.63 mm = 0.163 cm - Given, Meter Scale Reading = 0 Circular Scale Reading = 52 Least Count = $ \frac{1}{100} $ mm = 0.01 mm $$ d=MSR+CSR\times Least~Count $$ $$ =0+52\times0.01=0.52\mathrm{~mm} $$ $$ d=0.052cm $$ - Given, Least count = 0.01 mm No. of division on circular scale = 50 Pitch of the screw gauge = least count $ \times $ no. of divisions on circular scale = 0.01 $ \times $ 50 = 0.5 mm - Diameter of the ball = Meter Scale Reading + Circular Scale Reading $ \times $ (Least Count) - Zero error $$ 0.5+25\times0.001-(-0.004) $$ $$ =0.5+0.025+0.004 $$ $$ =0.529\ cm $$ - $ (N+1) $ vernier scale division (VSD) = (N) Main scale division $$ 1\ VSD=\left(\frac{N}{N+1}\right)MSD $$ $$ \begin{aligned}L.C.&=least~count=1MSD-1VSD\\&=\left(1-\frac{N}{N+1}\right)MSD\\&=\left(\frac{1}{N+1}\right)MSD\\&=\left(\frac{1}{N+1}\right)\times\frac{0.1}{10}cm\\&=\frac{1}{100(N+1)}cm\end{aligned} $$ - $n$ main scale divisions = 1 cm 1 main scale division = $\frac{1}{n}$ cm $$ 1\ M S D=\frac{1}{n}\ cm $$ $$ n\mathrm{VSD}=(n-1)\mathrm{MSD} $$ $$ 1\ VSD=\frac{n-1}{n}\ MSD $$ $$ \frac{1}{n}-\left(\frac{n-1}{n}\right) \mathbf{MSD} $$ $$ =\frac{1}{n}-\left(\frac{n-1}{n}\right)\left(\frac{1}{n}\mathrm{~cm}\right) $$ $$ \mathrm{L.C.}=\left(\frac{1}{n}-\frac{n-1}{n^{2}}\right)\mathrm{c m} $$ $$ L.C.=\frac{n-n+1}{n^{2}}=\frac{1}{n^{2}}cm $$ - The first and second resonance of tuning fork in resonance tube is shown below:
$$ \left(\begin{array}{c}\frac{1}{2}\\ \frac{3\lambda}{4}=l_{2}\end{array}\right)\left(\begin{array}{c}\frac{\lambda}{4}\\ \frac{\lambda}{2}\end{array}\right) $$ Here, $$ l_{1}=\frac{\lambda}{4}\text{and}l_{2}=\frac{3\lambda}{4} $$ $$ \Rightarrow $$ $$ l_{2}-l_{1}=\frac{3\lambda}{4}-\frac{\lambda}{4}=\frac{2\lambda}{4}=\frac{\lambda}{2} $$ $$ \lambda=2(l_{2}-l_{1}) $$ We know that, $$ \begin{aligned}\mathbf{v}&=f\boldsymbol{\lambda}\\\mathbf{v}&=800\times2\quad(31.25-9.75)\\&=34400\mathrm{~cm/s}\\\mathbf{v}&=344\mathrm{~m/s}\end{aligned} $$
##### ! Caution Students should remember that in second resonance use $ l_{2} = \frac{\lambda}{2} $ but it is wrong, the first, $ l_{1} = \frac{\lambda}{4} $ and then $ \frac{\lambda}{2} $ means $ l_{2} = \frac{\lambda}{4} + \frac{\lambda}{2} = \frac{3\lambda}{4} $ - Using ohm's law, $$ \mathrm{\frac{d V}{d l}}=-r $$ and $$ [\because\mathrm{~V~}+\mathrm{I}r=\mathrm{E}] $$ $$ \mathbf{V}=\mathbf{E}, $$ if $$ 1=0 $$ $ \therefore $ slope of graph = - r and Intercept = E - In the first case,
At balance point, $$ \mathrm{\frac{5}{R}}={\frac{l_{1}}{100-l_{1}}} $$ In the second case, At balance point. $$ \frac{5}{\left(\frac{\mathrm{R}}{2}\right)}=\frac{1.6l_{1}}{100-1.6l_{1}} $$
Divide eq. (i) by eq (ii), we get, $$ \frac{1}{2}\;=\;\frac{100-1.6\;l_{1}}{1.6(100-l_{1})} $$ $$ 160-1.6\ l_{1}=200-3.2\ l_{1} $$ $$ 1.6-1.6\ l_{1}=200-3.2\ l_{1}\\\ 1.6\ l_{1}=40\ or\ l_{1}=\frac{40}{1.6}=25\ cm $$ Substituting this value in eq (i), we get, $$ \mathrm{\frac{5}{r}} = \mathrm{\frac{25}{75}} $$ $$ \begin{aligned}R&=\frac{375}{25}\Omega\\&=15\Omega.\end{aligned} $$ - From prism formula, $$ \upmu=\frac{\sin\left(\frac{\mathbf{A}+\delta}{2}\right)}{\sin\left(\frac{\mathbf{A}}{2}\right)} $$ $$ \begin{aligned}\cot\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2}&=\frac{\sin\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}+\delta}{2}\right)}{\sin\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2}\right)}\\\Rightarrow\quad\frac{\cos\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2}\right)}{\sin\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2}\right)}&=\frac{\sin\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}+\delta}{2}\right)}{\sin\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2}\right)}\\\Rightarrow\quad\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2}\right)&=\sin\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2}+\frac{\delta}{2}\right)\\\Rightarrow\quad\delta&=\pi-2\mathrm{A}\end{aligned} $$ - It is V-I characteristics for solar cell where point A represents open circuit voltage and point B short circuit current. - The current in a $p-n$ junction is of the order of milli-ampere in forward biasing and of the order of micro-ampere in reverse biasing (negligible). Hence, we can conclude that the device is a $p-n$ junction. ### ТЕЛИ КОНИООР
